reason_for_decision: The article passes because it treats low-altitude security as a layered architecture that combines airspace context, sensing, fusion, command, and response integration.
strengths: The article is strong on elevating airspace context into the architecture rather than leaving it as an external data source.
It keeps fusion, command, and resilience in the core design instead of treating them as add-ons.
The sections on governance, interfaces, and degraded-mode validation give the piece real system-design depth.
summary_assessment: This is a high-quality pass. The article offers a mature architectural model for low-altitude security and stays well aligned with operational reality.
